title: Multi-Actor Service Discovery and Conflicting Outcomes · Claim Repair Board
summary: A preserved decision record for multi-actor service discovery and conflicting outcomes contains one plausible evidence, scope, design, implementation, evaluation, or ownership defect. Find the first unsupported claim before changing the product. Planned artifact: Repair a changed failure in multi-actor service map and conflict register and publish the reproduction, evidence, repair, and regression record. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debug…
objectives: Map direct users, indirect users, operators, approvers, maintainers, adversaries, and affected non-users with their decisions and dependencies.
Trace frontstage and backstage actions, handoffs, wait states, channels, policies, evidence, and breakdowns across an end-to-end service.
Expose conflicting speed, control, privacy, accessibility, workload, cost, and accountability outcomes instead of averaging them away.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.config:
# personal-project-2 cumulative independent product evidence portfolio
# Browser labs perform deterministic evidence-contract review only.
# They never contact participants or services, execute commands, deploy, or claim live conformance.
workspace: portfolio
project-id: personal-project-2
stakeholder: [replace with affected people and accountable decision owner]
decision: [replace with the decision this evidence changes]
problem-evidence: [replace with observed need, counterevidence, and uncertainty]
accessibility: [replace with keyboard, structure, reflow, status, alternatives, and human review]
security: [replace with threat, authorization, abuse, response, and residual risk]
privacy: [replace with purpose, minimization, retention, access, correction, and deletion]
test-strategy: [replace with behavior, changed case, fault, accessibility, security, and recovery]
release-evidence: [replace with revision, artifact, inventory, provenance, validation, and rollback]
recovery: [replace with backup, clean restore, reconciliation, communication, and ownership]
portfolio-proof: [replace with contribution, limitations, counterexample, and unseen defense]
transfer-boundary: authorized controlled research, toolchain, deployment, and review environments
evidence:

steps.title: Inspect the failure record · Debug Actor System Map
steps.instruction:
debug case — End users, operators, administrators, support staff, and affected non-users disagree about speed, control, workload, privacy, and recourse. The debug variation 3 changes the stakeholder, access need, evidence source, constraint, failure, and operating environment. Competency probe: Map direct users, indirect users, operators, approvers, maintainers, adversaries, and affected non-users with their decisions and dependencies.

Inspect the Actor System Map trace for this debug case. Which record connects a precondition, observed state change, and boundary result?

Evidence target: Inspect the failure record · Debug Actor System Map.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for map direct users, indirect users, operators, approvers, maintainers, adversaries, and affected non-users with their decisions and dependencies. For Inspect the failure record · Debug Actor System Map, keep Actor System Map connected to mechanically reviewable stakeholder, affected-user, artifact, changed-case, failure, repair, release, recovery, and transfer decision evidence.

content.text: For Actor System Map, distinguish a claim from evidence by locating the relevant precondition, trace or invariant, and changed input result.

hints: For Inspect the failure record · Debug Actor System Map, name the types, ownership, and stop path, Actor System Map precondition, or invariant that must hold.

At Inspect the failure record · Debug Actor System Map, trace this concrete input and predict values, errors, blocking, and output before reviewing it: End users, operators, administrators, support staff, and affected non-users disagree about speed, control, workload, privacy, and recourse. The debug variation 3 changes the stakeholder, access need, evidence source, constraint, failure, and operating environment. Competency probe: Map direct users, indirect users, operators, approvers, maintainers, adversaries, and affected non-users with their decisions and dependencies.

For Inspect the failure record · Debug Actor System Map, challenge the documented misconception with runtime, failure, race, or toolchain evidence: Only people who click the interface are product users.

stimulus.title: Actor System Map case record
stimulus.caption: Three excerpts from a learner evidence packet; only one connects a claim to a reproducible check.

lines.label: assumption
lines.text: End users, operators, administrators, support staff, and affected non-users disagree about speed, control, workload, privacy, and recourse. The debug variation 3 changes the stakeholder, access need, evidence source, constraint, failure, and operating environment. Competency probe: Map direct users, indirect users, operators, approvers, maintainers, adversaries, and affected non-users with their decisions and dependencies.

lines.label: risk
lines.text: Only people who click the interface are product users.

lines.label: evidence
lines.text: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Map direct users, indirect users, operators, approvers, maintainers, adversaries, and affected non-users with their decisions and dependencies. Artifact under review: multi-actor service map and conflict register.

options.text: The record names the assumption, applies Actor System Map, and reports a changed-case check tied to the stakeholder decision.

The Actor System Map record says multi-actor service map and conflict register looks right but supplies no governing condition, observation, changed case, or counterexample. That claim fails the inspect the failure record evidence boundary for Actor System Map.

For inspect the failure record, apply Actor System Map without its stated preconditions, omit the multi-actor service map and conflict register trace and counterexample, and treat one plausible output as complete proof. This does not satisfy Actor System Map for multi-actor service map and conflict register.

steps.title: Implement the cause-level repair · Debug Research Saturation Limit
steps.instruction:
Append a pp2-research-saturation-limit-1gik8u: manifest entry with concrete verify plus failure, owner, or repair evidence.

Evidence target: Implement the cause-level repair · Debug Research Saturation Limit.
steps.why: Building a runnable or mechanically reviewable artifact turns Research Saturation Limit into observable performance evidence. For Implement the cause-level repair · Debug Research Saturation Limit, keep Research Saturation Limit connected to mechanically reviewable stakeholder, affected-user, artifact, changed-case, failure, repair, release, recovery, and transfer decision evidence.

pp2-research-saturation-limit-1gik8u:

  failure: [replace with the rejected condition]
  repair: [replace with a local reproduction and correction path]
content.caption: Shape of the required increment; replace generic language with the current stakeholder case.

content.title: Evidence is more than syntax
content.text: The Research Saturation Limit structural check is only one gate. multi-actor service map and conflict register must also survive the stated changed case and causal explanation.
